Require the Municipal Health Officer to submit the lacking documents to support the payment of the Health Emergency Allowance (HEA) to Health Care Workers (HCWs) and Non-Health Care Workers (Non-HCWs) assigned in the Rural Health Unit and Barangay Health Stations for the period July 1 to December 31, 2022.

We commended the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned for continuously adhering to Section 13, 14 and 16 of Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 8291 to ensure that monthly premium contributions and loan repayments withheld during the year are fully remitted to GSIS.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
Review the paid payrolls for salaries and conduct monthly reconciliations of employee premium contributions and loan amortizations by comparing the GSIS billing with the actual payroll deductions and remitted amounts. This process ensures billing accuracy, facilitates timely remittances, and guarantees correct salary deductions.
Verify the non-moving GSIS and Pag-IBIG payables in a lump amount of P3,524.70 under SL No. 2-99-99-990-0009, reconcile the unsubstantiated balance, and implement necessary adjustments as warranted. Any confirmed outstanding balance must be promptly remitted to GSIS.
Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and AAO to update personnel’s employment status and/or salary adjustments. This will ensure that GSIS records reflect accurate and up-to-date information; and
Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to:
Obtain updated service records of all employee members from the Human Resource Officer and update the Agency Remittance Advice for submission to GSIS.
Make representation with GSIS to facilitate the reconciliation of records on the unsubstantiated balance of P3,524.70 under SL No. 2-99-99-990-0009.

We commended the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned for continuously adhering to Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that development projects under the 20% DF are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council to:
Instruct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, and other implementing offices to expedite and oversee the execution of the identified PPAs, utilizing 20% of the Development Fund, to ensure the fund's objectives are met.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide periodic updates to the Municipal Mayor and implementing offices on the SAAOB of the 20% DF and identify excess funds of completed projects and recommend the reversion and made available for re-appropriation;
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to make proposals to the Municipal Development Council on the realignment of appropriations for PPAs, sourced from Continuing Appropriation funds of 20% DF, which are no longer considered priority projects or are unable to be executed due to reasons beyond the control of Municipal Officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO and the LDRRM Council to fully utilize the LDRRMF by implementing all the identified PPAs in the LDRRMFIP encompassing all four (4) thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management in compliance with the relevant guidelines.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the LDRRMO, assist and guide the beneficiaries in submitting proof of housing repair and rehabilitation accomplishments, to ensure that the funds are utilized for their intended purpose.

We commended the efforts of the Municipality to insure its motor vehicle against various risks such as accidents, theft, fire, and even acts of nature.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO-Designate to:
Ensure that the consolidated Property Inventory Form includes all insurable properties of the Municipality, with details on their latest appraised values/valuation, and appraisal dates.
Submit the consolidated PIF to the Audit Team Leader and the GIF, GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year as required in COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 as basis for the assessment of the premium due for the general insurance coverage of all insurable assets, properties and interests of the Municipality with the GIF of the GSIS.

We commended the Municipal Mayor and all concerned personnel for their continued adherence to Republic Act No. 11223, ensuring that employee members' monthly premium contributions are fully remitted to PhilHealth.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with PhilHealth in facilitating the reconciliation of records concerning the unrecorded remittances for November 2015, September 2016, and October 2016.

We commended the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned for continuously adhering to Section 1, Rule VII of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9679 to ensure that monthly contributions and loan repayments withheld during the year were fully remitted to HDMF.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
Coordinate with HDMF to ensure the timely receipt of monthly billing statements.
Review the paid payrolls for salaries and conduct monthly reconciliations of employees’ contributions and loan amortizations to HDMF by comparing the actual payroll deductions with remitted amounts. This will ensure billing accuracy, facilitate timely remittances, and guarantee the correctness of salary deductions.
Verify the non-moving GSIS and Pag-IBIG payables in a lump amount of P3,524.70 recorded under SL No. 2-99-99-990-0009 of the Other Payables account, reconcile the unsubstantiated balance, and implement necessary adjustments as warranted.
Municipal Treasurer to:
Review the Bills Payment Report for each remittance made to HDMF through the Online Payment Facility via Virtual Pag-IBIG, ensuring that the payment has been successfully posted.
Coordinate with HDMF to facilitate the reconciliation of records regarding the unsubstantiated balance of P3,524.70 under SL No. 2-99-99-990-0009 of the Other Payables account.  Any confirmed outstanding balance must be promptly remitted to HDMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor Direct the members of the GFPS Technical Working Group (TWG) and GAD Focal Person to:
Empower the GAD Focal Point System to enhance their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related tools among others; and require the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the GAD Agenda in accordance with the mandate of 4.C.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
Plan the Municipality's activities aimed at achieving women's empowerment and addressing gender equality, focusing on priority projects while considering the Municipality's limited resources in terms of funds, time, manpower, and facility availability.
Refrain from implementing non-GAD-related activities and coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that expenses excluded from the GPB and those not clearly addressing a specific gender issue are not charged to the GAD Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), submit the certifications stating that the trainings attended and the travels undertaken by the Job Order personnel were (a) official missions/tasks that could not be performed by/or assigned to any other regular/permanent official and (b) the tasks/activities are necessary to fulfill the obligations as contained in his/her contract of service, duly approved by the Head of Agency and/or any authorized official.  This is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension, if warranted.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the travel claims of JO personnel to ensure they are approved by the authorized official and supported by the required certification and supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing payment.

We recommended that the Local School Board and the Municipal Accountant comply with the reportorial requirements on the transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the Special Education Fund as required in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2024-021 and Item 6.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

responsible in the accounting and reporting of the Local Road Network of the

determine and assess the cost of the road lot component of all municipal

Municipal Treasurer/ General Services Office with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components of each road

3. The Municipal Treasurer/ GSO prepare and maintain the Report of Local
Road Network (RLRN and Local Road Network Property Card
(LRNPC).

1. Record the cost of each road component after determination by the

2. Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Leger Card (LRNLC)

and provide information’s in the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) with regard
to Stock No., Re-order Point, date of the JEV/RIS, Reference, Issuance, and
Balance-Qty., as prescribed in LGU NGAS Instructions Volume 2, A-07 SLC,
that will easily establish the existence and accuracy of inventories

concerned to:

purpose, as outlined in the Memorandum of Agreement, and ensure it is
used within the validity period.

in implementing the PPAs.

maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for the "Due to NGA" account, in compliance

reconciled with the corresponding control account in the General Ledger.

concerned to consistently follow the guidelines outlined in the DBM-DOF-
DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, issued on November 4, 2020.
4.13 Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Budget Officer monitor the payment of the unpaid obligations.

reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the
said agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to
update the records of the GSIS.

Electronic Remittance File (ERF) Handlers to reconcile monthly balances
and promptly act on any discrepancies.

with the GSIS Billing Statements and the actual amount remitted to
avoid discrepancies.
2. Maintain a monitoring report detailing the ending balance of the Due
to GSIS, including the names of employees and their respective balance

1. Reconcile the recorded payroll deductions with the PhilHealth
remittance on a monthly basis to avoid further discrepancies;
2. Maintain a monitoring report that details the ending balance of the "Due to
PhilHealth" account, including the names of employees and their respective
balance statuses, to facilitate reconciliation;
3. Trace and verify the over remittances and under remittances listed in
Annex H; and
4. Remit the withheld contributions as of end of CY 2024 immediately, in
compliance with pertinent laws, to avoid penalties.

8.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. Reconcile the recorded deductions from payroll with the Pag-ibig
remittance monthly to avoid more discrepancies;
2. Maintain a monitoring report detailing the ending balance of the Due
to Pag-ibig, including the names of employees and their respective
balance statuses to facilitate reconciliation; and
3. Immediately remit the withheld contributions as of end of CY 2024 to
comply with pertinent laws and avoid the risk of penalties or sanctions.

employees to secure a Pag-IBIG number as a mandatory requirement.

9.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the initiative to ensure that
all insurable LGU properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund
(GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance
with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002
dated May 31, 2018 to mitigate the risk of losses resulting from natural and
man-made calamities. Further, adequate funding should be allocated for the
insurance of all insurable properties in the ensuring years.
9.13 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer as the Employee-in-
Charge be directed to prepare and submit the Property Inventory Form (PIF)
to the GSIS not later than April 30 of each year. The PIF should
include a complete list of all insurable properties and other assets, indicating
their latest appraised values or valuation, appraisal dates, locations, and
other relevant information.

1. Formulate and adopt a three-year GAD Agenda which shall be the basis
for the annual formulation of PPAs to be included in the LGU GAD Plan
and Budget;
2. Prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report with reference to the Approved GAD Plan and Budget;
3. Submit a detailed utilization report to substantiate the actual cost or
expenditures for evaluation.
4. Submit justification on the difference between the actual cost or
expenditure and the budget per DILG Endorsed GPB.

11.10 Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant to ensure that
all claims for travelling expenses are fully supported with the required supporting documents, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Resolution No. 2021-044.

1. The Municipal Accountant stop the practice of transferring the unpaid
portion of the 20% DF projects in the Trust Fund;
2. The Municipal Treasurer:
a. Open a bank account dedicated to 20% Development Fund under
the General Fund; and
b. The Municipal Treasurer make a justification on excess of expenses
over the budget (Deficit) of the Special Education Fund. This is
without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with a copy of the SEF Budget as
their basis for disbursement, certification of availability of funds, and record
keeping, not later than seven (7) days after the approval of the SEF Budget.

annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B)
on SEF Utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO,
through appropriate channels. A copy should also be furnished to the local
Sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG within
the reglementary period, for quarterly reports no later than the 20th day
after the end of the quarter; and for annual reports, not later than the 15th
day of February of the following year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to retrieve the data and records and obtain from the Municipality’s depository banks the related documents to support the recording of the variances of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account amounting to P25,984,808.09. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the other Department Heads, to exhaust all possible means and continue to exert utmost effort to reconcile the prior years’ variances of P15,730,469.18 that remained unaccounted as of December 31, 2024. Further, substantiate the negative/debit balances of the accounts namely, Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG, totaling P1,319,046.55.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to, henceforth, follow the required timeframe on the utilization and disbursement of a particular fund transfer for the implementation of a particular project. Otherwise, cause the return of the excess or any unutilized amount of fund transfer to the Bureau of Treasury or to the appropriate Source Agencies after the lapse of the required timeframe.

Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the collections to be remitted to the Bureau of Treasury, collections to be remitted to the Office of the Department of Interior and Local Government and fund downloaded by the National Government to the Bureau of Fire and Protection, totaling P78,391.05, originally recorded under the “Due to NGA” account to the “Trust Liability” Account in accordance with COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;

Exert utmost effort to verify the fund transfers without supporting documents amounting to P2,650,052.49 and send confirmation letters once the NGA will be identified to determine if the LGU have unsettled accounts. Also, coordinate with relevant personnel and Department Heads to verify fund transfer which were not duly supported with documents. 

c.	Henceforth, prepare regularly the Aging of Fund Transfer on a quarterly basis and conduct regular and periodic verification of the existence of the fund transfer.

2.	Municipal Treasurer to:

a.	Return the fund transfer amounting to P4,083,350.00 to the National Treasury in compliance with the DBM-LBC No. 140 dated December 28, 2021 which required that fund should be used only until December 31, 2022. Henceforth, return to the appropriate Source Agencies any excess or any unutilized amount of fund transfer after the lapse of the timeframe/period to implement a particular project.

b.	Liquidate immediately the fund transfer relative to the Ayuda sa Kapos ang Kita Program (AKAP) Rice Assistance amounting to P5,000,000.00 and cause the return of any excess or any unutilized amount of fund transfer in consonance with the Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the Office of the Department of Social Welfare and Development.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:

a. 	Human Resource and Management Officer to inform/provide the report/document to the Municipal Treasurer and the Payroll Maker relative to the changes/adjustment in the salaries of the employees;

c. 	Account for the difference between the premiums remitted and withheld amounting to P22,049.73 as presented in Table 12, otherwise remit the unremitted premiums, if warranted.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to issue a Memorandum/Order to enforce the P5,000.00 net take home policy among the employees of the LGU in compliance with Section 56 of the General Appropriations Act of CY 2025. 

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the:

a. 	Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, Human Resource Officer and Payroll Maker to stop the practice of certifying loan application of employees whose net take home pay falls below the minimum take home pay of P5,000.00; and

b. 	Payroll Maker/Municipal Accountant to issue Certificate of Net Take Home Pay as a requirement prior to the certification/confirmation/approval of loan application.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all insurable LGU properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) to protect the Municipality against any damage or loss of its properties and the risk of non-indemnification, in compliance with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate an Employee-in-Charge responsible in obtaining insurance coverage for the Municipality’s insurable properties with the GSIS. Further, instruct the Employee-in-Charge to prepare and submit to the GSIS the Property Inventory Form GSIS listing of all the insurable properties and other assets, showing their latest appraised values/valuation, appraisal date, location, and other information not later than April 30 of each year.

We reiterate our previous audit recommendations that earnest effort to implement all the approved PPAs should principally come from the Municipal Mayor being the head of agency identified as the responsible person in the Joint Memorandum Circular from DBM-DOF-DILG.

Likewise, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to:

a. 	Address the issues affecting the delay on the bidding-related activities and ensure that all approved PPAs are procurement and implementation-ready and warrant the implementation of all approved PPAs for the optimal utilization of the appropriation for development projects; and

b. 	Continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved and for the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local School Board furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the SEF Budget not later than seven (7) days after its approval, to serve as the basis for disbursements and for recording purposes.

We further recommended that the Local School Board submit quarterly and annual reports on SEF Utilization, prepared by the Municipal Accountant, to the DepEd Central Office, and copy furnish the local sanggunian concerned, as well as the Department of Budget and Management and the Department of the Interior and Local Government Regional Offices, not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter for quarterly reports, and not later than the 15th day of February for annual reports.

We commended the efforts of the Municipal Accountant in the conduct of thorough examination/analysis/review of the financial statements and verification of the books of accounts in Calendar Year 2023. Further, we also commended his effort in the reconciliation of certain material discrepancies and significant variances emanating from previous years. Nevertheless, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the other Department Heads, to exhaust all possible means and continue to exert utmost effort to reconcile the prior years’ variances that remained unaccounted as of December 31, 2023.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

Require the GSO to maintain property cards and update records regarding acquisition, description, custody, disposal, and other information about the PPE, based on the source documents of the transactions, and reconcile with the accounting records and RPCPPE.

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELCs and update records regarding acquisition, description, custody, estimated useful life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the PPE, based on the source documents of the transactions, and reconcile with the property records and RPCPPE.

Instruct the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant to:

Conduct the physical count of PPE, and implement the One-Time Cleansing of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; 

Undertake the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

Cause the submission of a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommend the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Health Officer, to: 

2. attach the complete supporting documents to the payrolls/vouchers. We likewise recommend the Municipal Accountant to process disbursement vouchers only when they are supported with complete documentation, and adhere strictly to the guidelines on the grant of hazard pay, subsistence, and laundry allowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Peace and Order Council, to direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:

Conduct a thorough review of the AIP of the LGU incorporating therein detailed list of PPAs and their corresponding appropriation for inclusion in the local government budget; and 

Utilize the POPS fund according to the approved AIP, Annual Budget, POPS plan to implement PPAs which directly contribute to the maintenance of the peace and order and public safety of the community.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that earnest effort to implement all the approved PPAs should principally come from the Municipal Mayor being the head of agency identified as the responsible person in the Joint Memorandum Circular from DBM-DOF-DILG.
Likewise, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the MPDC to:

Address the issues affecting the delay on the bidding-related activities and ensure that all approved PPAs are procurement and implementation-ready and warrant the implementation of all approved PPAs for the optimal utilization of the appropriation for development projects; and

Continuously adhere to the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020 to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcome of the Municipality is achieved and for the realization of the intended purpose of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC Chairperson to:

Require submission of the latest Business Tax Returns within five calendar days from receipt by the bidder of the notice from the BAC that the bidder has the Lowest Calculated Bid or Highest Rated Bid.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

2. Instruct the Inventory Committee to:

a. 	Formulate a Physical Inventory Plan and schedule for the conduct of physical inventory that will complement with the time of the members of the committee in order not to prejudice the operations of the Municipality. Also, conduct the physical count pursuant to 6.1 and 6.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

b. 	Cause the submission of a complete Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year.

3. 	Require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. 	Trace the differences in the account balances and reconcile her records with the financial statements; 

b. 	Act on the preparation and maintenance of the PPE ledger cards based on the source documents of the transactions; and

c. 	Provide the necessary disclosures required in IPSAS 17 on PPE.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the differences among the details/schedule, General Ledger, Subsidiary Ledger, Statement of Financial Position, and Notes to Financial Statement of the Due to NGA account as required by Section 121 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Paragraph 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the differences among the General Ledger, Subsidiary Ledger, Statement of Financial Position, and Notes to Financial Statement of the Due to LGU account and draw an adjusting entry to record the cash donations amounting to P5,017,350.00 to the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account instead of the Due to LGU account.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that:

b. 	The BAC to fully comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised IRR, specifically on the Annex E, which recommended the earliest possible time and maximum period allowed for the procurement of infrastructure projects on the issuance of notice of award, notice to proceed, contract preparation and signing, among others; 

c. 	The BAC Secretariat to submit all the necessary documents supporting the procurement which includes the post-qualification requirements, posting requirements, among others; and

d. 	Henceforth, strictly comply with all the provisions of RA 9184 and the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the:

2. 	Municipal Assessor to:

a. 	Update and reconcile records relative to real properties within the Municipality and its owners;

c. 	Conduct physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real properties within the Municipality are properly listed in the assessment rolls.







3. 	Municipal Treasurer to:

a. 	Reconcile the updated tax payer’s index card with the records of the Municipal Assessor;



b. 	Submit the Tax payer’s index card to the Municipal Accountant at the start of year as basis in recording RPT receivables; and

c. 	Develop collection strategies and intensify the collection of real property taxes and special education taxes to provide additional sources of funds of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council ensure that the municipality is prepared for calamities as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 by procuring standard disaster response equipment required in DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor commission the General Services Officer and Municipal Assessor or create a team to:

Gather and secure all the required legal documents necessary for registration such as but no limited to original deed of sale, deed of donation, original certificate of titles, etc; and

Cause the registration and transfer the land in the name of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Assessor make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds to be used for the expenses in processing the Transfer Certificate of Title.

We recommended that the Municipal Social and Development Officer together with the Property Supply Officer:

Conduct physical count of all the remaining donated items

Prepare inventory list of the remaining donated items for DRRM every month or every quarter or until all donations are fully distributed; and

Henceforth, prepare all the reports and documents required to account and monitor the receipt and issuances of all donations in kind and submit a copy of all the reports to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Supply Officer furnish the Municipal Accountant with the duplicate copies of all Acknowledgement Receipts of in-kind donations and inventory list of donated items for DRRM.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant summarize data from the documents submitted by the MSWDO and Supply Officer, compare records with the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM maintained by the MSWDO, and disclose the necessary information in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

Submit the lacking documentary requirements presented in Table 1, such as the Abstract of Quotation, BAC Resolution recommending award of contract to LCRB, Proof of Distribution to the Beneficiaries/Distribution List, and Liquidation Report. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension as a result of our audit; and

Henceforth, secure complete documentation on all disbursements to ensure the validity and propriety of the disbursement transactions. Significantly, adhere to the policy of non-payment of claims without complete documentation.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Local DRRM Fund;
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to charge depreciation annually on all depreciable assets in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 17.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to:

send within 10 days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the Mayor or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to request for the write off of the aforementioned accounts in accordance with COA Circular No, 2016-005 supported with an aging of dormant receivables and fund transfer, indicating in the remarks column the existence of the applicable column the existence of the applicable conditions, as follows:

a. Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or unreconciled reciprocal ac counts
b.	Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor
c.	Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor, and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her
d.	Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers
e.	No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to 

confirm with the GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PhilHealth the veracity of the negative balances and properly disclose the abnormal balances in the Notes to Financial Statements,

adapt monitoring mechanism to detect variances in the amount to be remitted and withheld, and 


accounting records such as cash receipts journal, check disbursement journal, cash disbursement journal, general and subsidiary ledgers must be updated monthly in order to determine the exact amount to be remitted to the GSIS, PhiHealth and PAG-IBIG.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a Status Report on the Revenue Code and coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan to look into the possible updating of the Revenue Code based on the report of the Municipal Treasurer.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the titling of untitled lots.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adopt stern regulations in the pursuit of zeroing-in the due and demandable cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in enforcing on the immediate settlement of audit disallowances.  Likewise, ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Solid Waste Management Focal Person to maintain disposal site records in the Residual Containment Area.

We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type of waste should be placed at the RCA, which will provide baseline data to assist in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for waste management.

We recommended that the RCA be fenced with a secured gate at the entrance, to minimize illegal visitors or scavengers.  This will also help address the occupational health and safety issues by limiting access.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of documents for the titling of lands owned by the Municipality.  We further recommend that before any construction is made, land ownership thru a certificate of title is obtained.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make reconciliation and verify who among the employees is deficient in paying the loan obligations.

the causes of the variance for proper accounting thereof;
b. compute and recognize depreciation on all of the depreciable assets of the
Municipality;
c. prepare and maintain PPELCs with complete significant information;
and
d. coordinate with the GSO-Designate/Supply Officer and undergo proper
and approved disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPE.
2. Require the GSO to prepare and maintain property cards with complete
significant information and reconcile the same with the records of the
Accounting Unit.

one-time physical inventory of all recorded PPE accounts will be conducted to verify
the existence of the properties. Also, depreciation expenses will be recognized upon

recorded. Coordination will be done for the proper disposition of the non-
existing/missing PPEs as well as the disposal of the unserviceable properties of the
LGU.

2.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a) Conduct a thorough review and validation of all long-outstanding accounts
payable, particularly those aged more than one year, to determine their
validity and whether the claims still exist and due for settlement;
b) Prepare adjusting journal entries to reclassify the amount of P147,569.00 as
Due to Officers and Employees and ultimately correct the balance of the
Accounts Payable account.

2.12 During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant committed to coordinate with
the suppliers and concerned personnel as to whether actual claims still exist, for
proper disposition. She likewise committed to prepare adjusting journal entries to
reclassify the employee benefits recorded as Accounts Payable to the Due to Officers
and Employees account.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile with PhilHealth
the prior years unremitted balance of PhilHealth contribution to establish the
actual existence of unremitted amount to PhilHealth.

3.8 We also recommended that, based on the results of the reconciliation,
appropriate adjusting entries be prepared if no unremitted PhilHealth
contributions are found to exist. Otherwise, the actual unremitted amount
should be promptly remitted to PhilHealth.

3.9 During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant stated that a representation was
already made with PhilHealth in CY 2023, as regards to the unremitted premiums of
the LGU which accumulated since CY 1988. She added that in CY 2025,
coordination will also be made with PhilHealth as to the proper treatment of the said
unremitted amounts and adjusting journal entries will be prepared subsequently,
where necessary.

4.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Payroll Officer:
1. Reconcile their records for the unremitted contribution in prior years; and
2. Coordinate with Pag-IBIG to match the Agency’s records vis-à-vis the
LGU to identify the cause of previous years’ unreconciled balances.

4.7 The Municipal Accountant explained that they have already taken steps on the
reconciliation of premiums and loan amortizations of previous years. She also cited
that necessary adjusting journal entries will subsequently be prepared as a result of
the reconciliation.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare adjusting journal entries to appropriately record the correct amount
of borrowing costs to be capitalized; and
b) henceforth, ensure adherence to IPSAS 5 regarding the accurate presentation
of asset and expense accounts in the financial statements.

6.11 We recommended that the BAC validate any negative slippage in the ongoing
contracts of the contractor to determine its cause. If the negative slippage is
found to be due to the contractor's fault, the contractor should be disqualified
pursuant to Section 34.3, Rule X of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

6.12 We also recommended that the BAC undertake the post-qualification
thoroughly to detect any misrepresentation in the bidding documents and
disqualify those contractors who are found ineligible to undertake the project.

6.13 During the exit conference, the BAC Chairman explained that they did not inspect or
validate further the discrepancy between the planned and actual accomplishment in
the Statement of All Ongoing Contracts of the contractors with lowest calculated and
responsive bid, during the post qualification, because based on LGU’s experience,
there are no negative slippage on projects undertaken by these contractors in the
LGU. Nonetheless, she committed to be more sensible and cautious in inspecting and
validating the bidders during post-qualification. Moreover, she cited that they will
add a personnel from the Engineering Office in the Technical Working Group as the
evaluation of projects needs their technical expertise.

9.10 We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer
communicate immediately to the GSIS any changes in the employees’ employment
status to update the records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium
contributions.

9.11 For future remittance, we recommended that the payroll officer accurately compute
the amount of deduction of loan amortization in the payroll to match the amount
billed by the GSIS.

9.12 The Payroll Officer commented during the exit conference that the variances will be
thoroughly reviewed including the unremitted premiums of previous years.

10.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the initiative to ensure that
all insurable LGU properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund
(GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance
with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002
dated May 31, 2018 to mitigate the risk of losses resulting from natural and
man-made calamities.

10.10 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer as the Employee-in-
Charge to prepare and submit the Property Inventory Form (PIF) containing
the list of all the insurable properties and other assets to the GSIS, showing
their latest appraised values/valuation, appraisal date, location, and other
information not later than April 30 of each year.

12.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Human Resource
Management Officer ensure that travel allowances are granted to JO personnel
whose contracts explicitly authorize official travel.

12.10 Moreover, in the absence of stipulation in the contract as to the travel authority,
we recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order
establishing internal guidelines on travel claims for JO personnel or in the
absence thereof, issue an Approved Travel Order stipulating the purpose of
travel with the corresponding entitlements. Furthermore, the travel claims must
be clearly specified in the contract and shall in no case exceed the rates and
amounts prescribed under Executive Order No. 77, series of 2019; and

12.11 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the
travel claims of JO personnel to ensure that the same are supported with
complete documentation, which includes a Certification from the Head of the
Agency or any authorized approving official as required in Item 5 of the Specific
Guidelines per COA Resolution No. 2021-044.

12.12 During the audit exit conference, the officials agreed to include an explicit stipulation
in the JO contracts as to their travel claims, which shall in no case exceed the rates
and amounts prescribed under Executive Order No. 77, series of 2019. The municipal
administrator also concurred on the issuance of internal guidelines on travel claims
through an Executive Order. Also, a memo will be issued on the additional
documentary requirements for travel.

13.12 We recommended that the Local School Board comply with the timely
submission of the SEF Approved Budget to the Municipal Accountant,
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer which should not be later
than seven days after the approval of the said Budget.

13.13 Also, we recommended that the Local School Board diligently comply with the
reportorial requirements on the submission of the SEF Utilization
Reports to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the
local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG,
in consonance with Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1,
s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017.

14.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amounts taxes withheld on government
payments as against the actual remittances made to address the identified
under or over-remittances and make adjustments, where necessary; and
2. Municipal Treasurer’s Office to strictly remit the taxes withheld in full and
within the period prescribed under the Revenue Regulations.

14.11 The Municipal Accountant committed that thorough review and reconciliation will
be undertaken. The payroll officer also assured the Audit Team that all the taxes
withheld will be properly remitted within the prescribed timeline.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor order the:
GSO-Designate to maintain and update records regarding acquisition, description, custody, disposal, and other information about the PPE, based on the source documents of the transactions, and reconcile with the Accounting Records and RPCPPE.
Municipal Accountant to update the record regarding acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information of PPE in the PPELCs and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in General Ledger and the Financial Statements.
Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant:
To complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period for one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts.
Prepare a report on the result of the physical count through RPCPPE including list of unserviceable PPEs, missing PPEs, and items found at station.
Any discrepancy between the RPCPPE, PPELCs, and PCs shall be investigated, verified, and adjusted. The total reconciled balances for each PPE account shall be established as the correct balance of PPE at year-end; and;
to undertake the disposition of the non-existing and unserviceable PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Temporary relief of the Inventory Committee from their daily task to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed.
We further recommended the Municipal Mayor to cause the conduct of an investigation to determine person accountable, circumstances of the loss, and the persons responsible for the loss, among others, and demand shall be made from the accountable personnel to produce the item or pay the depreciated replacement cost

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the department heads' compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer together with the Municipal Assessor:
Trace and assess every component of the Road Networks such as road lots, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures, and other miscellaneous structures and submit the report to the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer.
Assess the local road at every reporting date, whether there is any indication that a component of the local road may be impaired.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost as per report of the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor.
Prepare and maintain Road Network Subsidiary Ledgers and separate subsidiary ledgers with complete description for each road and its components, such as road lots, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures, and other miscellaneous structures and reconcile with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map to ensure completeness.
Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card, Annex D of COA Circular 2015-008.
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year over the prescribed useful life.
Provide disclosures on the total network system in the Notes to Financial Statements (Annex B of the Circular).
Finally, we recommended that the General Services Officer-Designate to:
Render a report on Local Road Network - Annex A of the Circular, at the end of the accounting period;
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card tor all roads and its components, (Annex E) of the Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant:
prepare the appropriate journal entry voucher to transfer the cost of completed projects from the Construction in Progress account to its proper Property, Plant and Equipment account, and to correct recorded cost of the project Completion of Multi-Purpose Building – Pung-on, Carnaga; and
ensure that entries posted in the General Ledgers are properly recorded in the Cash or Check Disbursement Journals and General Journals.
Municipal Engineer immediately issue the Certificates of Completion of projects upon its completion and inspection, and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis of the latter for the transfer of completed projects to the PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant:
Reconcile the beginning balance of Due to BIR account and make the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, conduct regular reconciliation of the unremitted balances.
Strictly withhold taxes when income payment is paid or payable, or the income payment is accrued or recorded as an expense or asset, whichever is applicable, in the payor's books, whichever comes first and remit the taxes withheld within the period prescribed under the Revenue Regulations.
Communicate with the Internet Provider for the withholding of CWT and VAT and the issuance of Invoice for every payment made.
Require all professionals and contract-of-service workers to submit the appropriate sworn declarations before processing any payments.
Withhold taxes at the prescribed rates for the income payments made to the speakers and other professional fees regardless of the mode of disbursement/ payment.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor order the Municipal Treasurer for the immediate remittance of the GSIS Contributions and Loan Amortizations based on the reconciled data of the Municipal Accountant.
We likewise recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer:
reconcile records with the GSIS and promptly update any changes in employment status and salary adjustments in the GSIS Web MPS and in the prescribed GSIS format to ensure timely and accurate record updates.
monitor the approval of employees' loan applications and immediately inform the person-in-charge of the payroll and Municipal Accountant to ensure timely deductions of loan repayments from the payroll.
Also, we recommended that the Office-in-charge of the preparation of payroll ensure that all premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted from employees' salaries in the payroll for the month they are due.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
provide the Audit Team with an explanation of the discrepancies identified in the GSIS billing, the amounts withheld from contributions and loan deductions, and the corresponding remittances; and
henceforth, conduct monthly reconciliations of employee premium contributions and loan amortizations by comparing GSIS billings with actual payroll deductions and remitted amounts. This practice ensures billing accuracy, facilitates timely remittances to GSIS, and guarantees correctness of deductions from the salaries of employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the reconciliation on the Pag-IBIG Contributions and determine the correct amount of contributions to be remitted in CY 2024, and the immediate remittance of the amount withheld from employees and the government share for Pag-IBIG Contributions, including the deficiencies.
We further recommended that the Office of the Human Resource Management shall ensure that the records in the Pag-IBIG Fund are updated and the amount to be withheld in the payroll shall reconcile with the amount to be remitted as per the system of Pag-IBIG Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the employee in-charge of the remittance, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to thoroughly account for the beginning balance of the "Due to PhilHealth" account. Any necessary adjustments or corrections should be properly recorded, and all unremitted contributions must be promptly remitted.
Additionally, we recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer reconcile municipal records with PhilHealth and ensure timely updates of employees' employment status and salary adjustments in the Electronic Premium Remittance System (EPRS). The monthly PhilHealth contributions reflected in the EPRS should consistently match the amounts withheld and the LGU’s counterpart contribution.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a monthly reconciliation of PhilHealth contributions against the Preliminary Employees Premium Remittance List before processing the Disbursement Voucher for remittance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take proactive measures to ensure that all insurable LGU properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002 to mitigate the risk of losses resulting from calamities and unforeseen events.  Also:
Designate a person in-charge of the preparation of Property Inventory Form and the submission thereof to the COA Supervising Auditor/ Audit Team Leader and the GSIS not later than April 30 of each year; and
Determine the latest appraised value of all insurable properties. An in-house appraisal shall be sufficient if the property or insurable interest has a value of P10 million and below. Otherwise, an independent appraisal shall be necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Chairperson of the Municipal DRRM Council (MDRRMC) and the MDRRMO ensure that disbursements charged under the fund align with eligible disaster preparedness, response, and recovery efforts as enumerated per thematic areas of DRRM under the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor to augment any item in the approved annual budget from savings in other items within the same expense class of their respective appropriations.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer:
Strictly adhere to the approved annual budget and ensure that all expenditures are supported with available appropriation; and
Monitor the changes in the annual budget based on the approved Supplemental Budget and any budget realignments approved by the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), or by the Municipal Mayor as authorized by the SB, through an Ordinance.
We finally recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the MDRRMO submit justification on the disbursements made without or in excess of the appropriations.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point    System-TWG to:
Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues.
Prepare a GAD Agenda identifying priority gender issues based on the GAD-related laws and policies, results of gender analysis and/or gender assessment, and results of program and project evaluation in terms of benefits to target beneficiaries.
Formulate GAD Plan and Budget based on the GAD Agenda and in accordance with the latest guidelines prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2024-01, dated December 2, 2024.
Enhance knowledge and skills in gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related tools through participation in relevant GAD training programs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor clearly and specifically state in the Travel Order the entitlements of the Job Order to travel expenses on travels authorized to be undertaken.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
strictly observe compliance with the provisions of Executive Order No. 77 on the rates and apportionment of daily travel expenses; and
refrain from processing disbursement vouchers on travel claims of Job Order personnel without an approved Travel Order, and the additional Certification from the Head of the Agency or any authorized approving official as required in Item 5 of the Specific Guidelines per COA Resolution No. 2021-044.

We recommended that the Local School Board furnish copies of the approved SEF Budget to the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer within seven (7) days from its approval. Furthermore, the quarterly and annual reports on SEF Utilization, prepared by the Municipal Accountant, should be submitted to the DepEd Central Office, with copies furnished to the concerned local sanggunian and the Regional Office of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM), not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter for quarterly reports, and not later than the 15th day of February for annual reports.

We recommended that the:

City Accountant -

1. record adjustments corresponding to the noted accounting errors and omissions based on relevant and sufficient documents; and

coordinate with the City Treasurer for the timely submission of reports and regular reconciliation of records.

b.  City Treasurer-

submit to the OCA all financial reports and documents promptly in accordance with existing regulations; and

coordinate with the City Accountant for the regular reconciliation of records.

observations.reason: Four projects implemented in CY 2024 lacked sufficient detailed engineering (see finding No. 3, Part II of this report)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management evaluate the feasibility of the project. If implementation proves to be doubtful or no longer necessary, it may consider preparing a different request or proposal.  Otherwise, said funds shall be returned to DOLE to relieve the City of obligations outlined in the MOA.

We recommended that Management:

fully utilize the automated teller machine payroll system for payment of employee compensation and benefits; and

discontinue the practice of paying expenses out of the cash advances when they could be practically paid through checks in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer regarding the existence of the stale checks.  The latter should send written notices to the concerned payees before checks become stale.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjustment in its books of accounts for stale checks.  Moreover, for checks that become stale and need replacement, a new check may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the Municipal Treasurer.  The replacement check shall also be chronologically reported in the RCI prepared at the time of cancellation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have been instructed to the monitor checks issued.  Adjustments have been made for stale checks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to identify the creditors comprising the accounts payable as of CY 2023 to establish the propriety and the validity of the accounts payable; otherwise, the same should be reverted if no legitimate claims exist.

We also recommended further that the Municipal Accountant submit an aging of accounts payable or schedules of the same account as an attachment in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2023.

Finally, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that such aging of accounts payable or supporting schedules will serve as additional attachments for the FS to be prepared in the succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: The creditors have been identified and aging have been prepared.  Moving forward, this office will include the aging in the FS for the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustment in its books of accounts for transactions involving the 10% retention money recorded under Other Payable Account. 

We recommended further, as a future reference, see to it that the 10% retention money be recorded under the Guaranty Deposit Payable Account.

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant should strictly observe the accounting policy on the proper account title to be used in every transaction of the Municipality in line with the foregoing government accounting manual.
observations.managementAction: The office have already prepared adjusting entries to correct the errors and have already used the proper account as prescribed by COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Special Disbursing Officer, submit within 10 days from receipt of the AOM, (i) the liquidation reports and its supporting documents to the ICFAU within the prescribed period, in a sealed envelope with a visible label “CONFIDENTIAL – For ICFAU Only”; and (ii) liquidation reports for the unliquidated cash advances totaling ₱53,262.52 and their respective supporting documents. Otherwise, restitute/return immediately the amount equivalent to the respective unliquidated cash advances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that all cash advance vouchers and complete required supporting documents are submitted within seven days after release of check, in a sealed envelope, to the Audit Team for post audit; (ii) record the liquidation in the books within five days after the receipt of the copy of the transmittal of documents duly acknowledged by the ICFAU or effect adjusting entries for the restitution/ return of unliquidated cash advances, as the case may be; and (iii) ensure that officials/employees with unliquidated cash advances shall not be granted another cash advance in accordance with the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

Lastly, we recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 to ensure proper budgeting, utilization and reporting of the confidential funds.
observations.managementAction: The liquidation of the previous  cash advance for confidential fund have been liquidated by the disbursing officer as evidenced by the received transmittal letter to ICFAU.  Secondly, the vouchers and attachments for this year was already submitted to the ATL within the prescribed period as evidenced by the transmittal letter to COA ATL.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MSWDO:

To submit the noted lacking supporting documents and strictly observe the required documentation prescribed under the foregoing laws, rules, and regulations; and

As a future reference, examine meticulously the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization, liquidation, and reporting of financial assistance received from the National Government Agencies.  In addition, we recommend further to submit the required monthly reports on fund utilization together with the relevant supporting documents in compliance with the existing law, rules, and regulations
observations.managementAction: Lacking documents have been submitted.  Also, moving forward, our office will carefully checks all MOAs of funds coming from other agencies for proper utilization and liquidation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to:

prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year including reasons for such non-collection; and

submit such list to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, and request for assistance from the SB in the enforcement of the remedies for collection provided in RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: We will adhere to the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource and Management Officer to: 

review, assess and evaluate the existing manpower, in coordination with the department heads, to prepare an updated Organizational Structure/Staffing Pattern Plan so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established and submit a report with recommendations thereon to the Local Chief Executive; 

formulate uniform policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs, taking into consideration the existing regular employees; and

incorporate in the contract the number of man hours, quantity and duration of the work to be performed by the JO personnel and require them to submit quantified accomplishment reports to support payments of wages.
observations.managementAction: We will adhere to the recommendations.
observations.reason: Report have been submitted to the mayor and waiting for actions from his office.

observations.recommendation: Send demand letters to the Barangay Officials concerned to submit certification under oath that documents pertaining to projects implemented from fund transfers were destroyed/lost to typhoon Yolanda or other fortuitous events, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Prepare demand letters and send to all barangays that have unliquidated fund transfers from LGU.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer confer with the bank pertaining to its LBP account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer already conferred with LBP and requested certification of the said bank account balance as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: 2. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant update regularly its subsidiary ledgers for cash-in-bank accounts to facilitate bank reconciliation and preparation of its respective monthly bank statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer submitted to the bank its requirements in the closing of the dormant bank account.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refer to Observation No. 2 of Part II and coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer, and record the transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF (QRF & MOOE) from the General Fund to the Trust Fund at yearend.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already implemented.
observations.reason: Not Reiterated in this Audit Report. Please refer to Observation No. 2 of Part II

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record progress payments to the appropriate construction in progress (CIP) accounts during the construction period, and close only such CIP account to its proper asset account upon its completion.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was already made per JEV No. TFGJ No. 2024-06-009

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, if unwilling to handle such confidential funds, designate the Municipal Treasurer as the SDO on cash advances for confidential funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor designated the Municipal Treasurer as the SDO on cash advances for confidential funds, and ensure its timely rendition of liquidation reports thereon.

observations.recommendation: 6. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that the required supporting documents are attached whenever cash advances are made on confidential funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that all required supporting documents are attached.

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommended that the LGU require all contractors to comply with the submission of the CSHP for the protection and welfare of the workers and the public. Moreover, proceed with contract signing only when all documents that are required part of the contract, particularly the CSHP, are complete and submitted to the BAC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor require all contractors to comply with the submission of the CSHP and proceed with contract signing only when all documents that are required part of the contract, particularly the CSHP, are complete and submitted to the BAC.

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) require the Municipal Assessor to submit the certified list of taxpayers to the Treasurer; and b) direct the Municipal Accountant to examine the history of the accounts of RPT and SET Receivables and its corresponding deferred accounts to identify the cause of the discrepancy of ₱3,053,261.09, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to resolve such discrepancy.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor had submitted the certified list of taxpayers to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Not Still in the process of reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) secure immediately an authorization from the Sanggunian Bayan, by way of ratification, for the transactions totaling ₱1,900,000.00 which were charged against lump-sum and generic appropriations of the 20% Development Fund; b) specify in the Annual Investment Plan, together with the Local Development Council, the PPAs to be funded from the 20% Development Fund and avoid lump-sum and generic appropriations therein, otherwise, obtain the corresponding SB Resolution prior to the implementation of such PPAs; and c) oblige the recipient barangays to submit liquidation documents to ensure that the funds released were only utilized for the development projects.
observations.managementAction: PPAs were indicated in the AIP and the Municipal Accountant recorded the grant of financial assistance out of the 20% Development Fund in the total amount of ₱1,900,000.00 in Due from LGUs and Prior Years’ Adjustment Accounts.
observations.reason: For the 2025 AIP, specific projects were indicated.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the heads of departments and operating units, assess the agency-wide personnel needs and create a responsive staffing pattern for the LGU, which shall be the basis in the hiring of Job Order personnel.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor, together with the heads of departments and operating units, shall assess the agency-wide personnel needs and create a staffing pattern for the LGU.
observations.reason: Not Still in the process of agency-wide assessment for the purpose of creating a responsive staffing pattern for the LGU.

observations.recommendation: 13. We also recommended that the Property Custodian prepare and maintain Property Cards for each class of PPE, and LRNPC for all roads and its components, based on the RPCPPE and RPCLRN, respectively; Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
observations.managementAction: Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit to the Audit Team. The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor to assist as specified.
observations.reason: Not Still in the process of gathering data for the derecognition.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant start the process for the filing of a request for write-off of dormant receivables to the Commission on Audit, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, after all remedies to collect the same have been exhausted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant start the process for the filing.
observations.reason: Not Still in the process of gathering the requirements for the request for write-off of dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: 18. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to cancel the stale checks and restore the amount to the Cash in Bank- LCCA account after it has been identified and documented.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher prepared and stale checks cancelled; amount restored.

observations.recommendation: 19. We strongly recommended that the Municipality implement the PPAs embodied in the MSWMP as provided in RA No. 9003 and DENR Administrative Order No. 2001-34 dated December 20, 2001. Likewise, require the MSWMB to submit an explanation or justification on why they should not be sanctioned by the law as provided in Section 50 and 52 of the same Act.
observations.managementAction: PPAs were integrated in the AIP this year, and will also be integrated in the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor expedite the hiring of the said vacant positions, and solicit some advice from the CSC in order to facilitate its immediate hiring.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor already appointed a Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: No applicants yet have applied for the position of Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation: 22. We recommend that management maximize, and optimize the utilization and maintenance of the farm tractor to support the agricultural activities of the farmer beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management implement measures to maximize utilization and maintenance of farm tractor.

observations.recommendation: 23. We recommend the Office of the Municipal Accountant to: Record the purchase of supplies and materials as Inventory in the corresponding Supplies Ledger Cards and update the General Ledger; 24. Record promptly the submitted Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued from the Supply Office through the preparation of a Journal Entry Voucher; 25. Reconcile Agricultural Supplies, Other Supplies, and Accountable Forms accounts in Balance Sheets as of December 31, 2014; 26. Record the purchase of supplies and materials in appropriate stock cards; 27. Require the end-user to fill-up a duly approved Requisition and Issue Slip upon issuance of supplies and materials; 28. Submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued every month to the Office of the Municipal Accountant as basis for the latter to record the issuance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Head of the Human Resource Management Office and the Municipal Budget Officer, formulate and implement appropriate measures to address the delayed release of wages for Job Order (JO) personnel.  Proper planning and budgeting for JO personnel should be strictly observed. Any additional hiring should be supported by adequate appropriation to prevent funding shortfalls and delays in salary payments.
Furthermore, we recommended that Management periodically assess the necessity and cost-effectiveness of hiring JO personnel to ensure that staffing requirements are met without compromising budgetary constraints.  In cases where additional hiring is deemed necessary, management should proactively initiate the timely preparation and approval of a supplemental budget to cover the corresponding expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The LGU submitted or formulated a supplemental budget for the augmentation of funds for FY 2026. The LGU appropriated funds intended for the whole year wages of Job Order Personnel. For this current year FY (2025) there is an approved supplemental budget worth 3,173,306.91 for the months of October-December 2025 Wages of job Order Personnel.

observations.recommendation:

We recommended that the Accounting Unit:
Conduct a detailed review of the LGU employees’ deductions against the remittances made to GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG Fund for premium contributions and loan amortization to reconcile the identified over-remittances totaling ₱38,875.08; and
Regularly review the schedule of premium remittance and loan repayments as deducted from personnel payroll to prevent under/over remittance of premium contributions and loan amortizations.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant meticulously review the payroll deductions recorded in the Due to GSIS, Due to PhilHealth, and Due to Pag-IBIG subsidiary ledgers. These records should be reconciled with the actual amounts payable to each agency before processing of payment to ensure accurate and complete remittance of all contributions.

Municipal Assessor- The team is currently conducting an on-going general revision of real property assessment that started last December 2022 and expected to complete until December 2023. In addition, updating and reconciling of real properties will be attended once the physical survey is completed. Lastly, since the Municipal Engineer is acting in concurrent capacity as Municipal Assessor- Designate, it would be difficult for her to devote full time in Assessor’s office, thus, immediate hiring of full time Municipal Assessor would be beneficial to the effective discharge of the relevant functions of the position.

Municipal Treasurer- Agree to reconcile the updated tax payer’s index card but will wait on the completion of general real property assessment revision conducted by the Assessor’s office. Also, to fast pace the process of reconciliation, MTO shall resume utilization of I-Tax System. On the other hand, we also agree to send the accountant tax payer’s index card every start of the year.
