observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have issued some demand letters. The Municipal Mayor will implement the sanctions provided for in COA Circular No. 97-002. A request for write-off will be filed to COA for the accounts of deceased employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer pay to the LGU the amount paid as penalty for late remittance of taxes withheld.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Officer ensured the recording in the book of accounts all remittable VAT and withholding taxes while various discrepancies in the recording and remittance of taxes withheld during the year were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer reconcile records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said agency any necessary changes in the year-end records on premium contributions. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource and Management Officer reconciled their records with the GSIS and communicated immediately to the said agency any necessary changes in the yearend records on premium contributions. The Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality prioritize the establishment of a Sanitary Landfill to achieve proper disposal of solid wastes including all barangays in the Municipality. Comply strictly with the minimum requirements in the operation of the LGU’s controlled dumpsite to safeguard environment and health of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still in the process of obtaining the Environmental Compliance Certificate. The Sanitary Landfill is already in the process of site development and processing for the issuance of ECC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: (48) provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs. (49) strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents, and (50) implement programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste management program.
observations.managementAction: The LGU have carried out and implemented information campaign on the no-littering policy and coordinate with barangays on the establishment of MRF. However, some barangays prioritized the centralized MRF and Sanitary Landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares a Journal Entry Voucher to record the identified reconciling items as of December 31, 2015; (52) for the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers identified in the bank reconciliation statement unrecorded, by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant; and (51) record the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Some reconciling items were already recorded. However there were still reconciling items which were not recorded due to the absence of bank statements and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU by the Inventory Committee and for the preparation of the Report on Physical Inventory of Property, Plants and Equipment. Reconciliation between the Inventory Report and the accounting records should be conducted by the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee and for the former to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect an accurate balance of the PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was created, however the Inventory Report could not be ascertained due to the management’s failure to conduct the annual physical inventory of all properties. The physical inventory of properties was conducted by the GSO officer. Reconciliation between the Inventory Report and the accounting records should be conducted by the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee and for the former to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect an accurate balance of the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance of the LDRRMF was already disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive ensure for timely submission of accounts monthly as mandated, in effect to protect concerned personnel against the legal consequences of non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: Accounts were still not submitted within the prescribed period. The Municipal Accountant committed to update accounts and to submit to COA within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that programs/projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund that were included in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) of the year should be implemented immediately in order to provide the constituents there immediate needs and for the attainment of targeted developments through the fund.
observations.managementAction: The LGU ensured that most of the programs/projects out of the 20% DF were implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation:
1.10  We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant:  (1)  trace  the  exact
    date of acquisition of the infrastructure assets acquired  in  CY  2016
    and CY 2017; (2) compute the depreciation of the  infrastructure  asset
    except for the road lot component of Road Network, and; (3) record  the
    necessary adjustments in order to reflect in the books of the  LGU  the
    correct carrying value of its Infrastructure Assets.

2.8   We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  record  subsequent  road
    projects in the Road Network account and prepare Journal Entry  Voucher
    to correct the projects erroneously classified  in  other  accounts  to
    Road Network account.

3.9   We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) record  purchase  of
    fuel, oil and lubricant in the Inventory account and  not  as  outright
    expense;   (2) require the submission of Fuel Consumption  Reports  and
    Driver’s Trip Tickets as basis for recording  issuances/utilization  of
    fuel; and (3) comply strictly with the provisions of COA  Circular  No.
    75-6 dated November 7, 1975 on fuel consumption.

4.10   We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  create  the  Inventory,
    Disposal and Appraisal  Committee  to  initiate  the  disposal  of  all
    unserviceable  and  damaged  properties  of  the  LGU   following   the
    procedures outlined in COA  Circular  No.  89-296  to  prevent  further
    deterioration and exposure to theft of these properties.

5.10  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

    (1) Require the Barangay Officials concerned to submit immediately  all
        Utilization  Reports  pertaining  to  funds  received  by  the  said
        barangays from the Municipality in prior years;
    (2) Desist from giving additional funds to  the  said  barangay  unless
        full liquidation of the previous releases are submitted, and;
    (3)     Require  the  recipient  barangays  to  sign  a  Memorandum  of
        Agreement with the Municipality, with stipulations on  the  specific
        purpose for the  fund  transfer  and  the  responsibilities  of  the
        recipients and other essential provisions.

5.11 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

    (1) Certify on the face of the DV that funds previously transferred  to
        the payee- Barangay has been liquidated and  accounted  for  in  the
        books;
    (2) Record immediately all liquidation of funds transferred  to  KALAHI
        CIDDS based on the documents submitted by the said organization;
    (3) Reclassify fund transfers to NGO/PO to Due from NGO/POs and require
        its immediate liquidation as well.

6.8   We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  prepare  the  necessary
    documents on the dormant receivable accounts with  evidences  that  the
    same could no longer be collected.

6.9   We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor  file  the  request  for
    write-off based on the documents submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

7.12  We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prioritize the  collection
    of the overdue receivables from consumers  and  furnish  to  Accounting
    copy of billing statements.

8.12   We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Engineer  conduct  a  Detailed
    Engineering Survey prior to procurement of all infrastructure  projects
    to include confirmation on the ownership of the  affected lots to avoid
    delay in the project implementation.

8.14  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  record  receivables
    pertaining to Waterworks System in  the  books  once  copy  of  billing
    statements are received, for a fair presentation of the accounts in the
    financial statements.

9.6   We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist shall:

     a. Demand copies of dated delivery receipts from source agencies of all
        delivered donations in-kind;
     b. Determine the cost of all delivered items;
     c. Forward copies of the delivery receipts and other documents relative
        to the delivered items to the Accounting Office for recording;
     d. Inform the source NGAs  regarding  the  capability  of  the  LGU  as
        beneficiary of the donations in-kind to avoid wastage of  government
        funds.


9.7    We  also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  record   all
    donations in-kind as Inventories so that accountability for these items
    could be established. When cost for recognition  cannot  be  determined
    the fair value of the inventories shall be used.

10.7  We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  reconcile  monthly  the
    actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing  and  against
    the amount remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.

11.7  We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

     a) Require from all accountable officers, the full liquidation of  cash
        advances, including the submission of  proof  of  return  of  excess
        amounts, within the reglementary period;

     b) Certify in the DV for each grant of cash advance that  the  previous
        cash advances of the claimant have  been  liquidated  and  accounted
        for;

     c) Grant cash advances at an amount sufficient for the purpose to avoid
        excess claims.

12.8  We recommended that:


     a) the Municipal Engineer, as the BAC Chairman require the contractors
        of on-going infrastructure projects to post additional  Performance
        Security to include the coverage period of one year from completion
        date;


     b) the BAC Secretariat examine  the  validity  period  of  Performance
        Security posted by winning bidders upon signing  of  the  contract,
        and ensure that the coverage period will include one year after the
        completion of the project, and;


     c) the Municipal Mayor issue the Certificate of Final  Acceptance  one
        year after the completion of each project.

13.8 We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor,  and  other  LGU  officials
    concerned, accelerate the  acquisition  of  a  lot  for  the  municipal
    sanitary landfill. If not  yet  available,  comply  strictly  with  the
    minimum requirements in the operation of the LGU’s controlled  dumpsite
    to safeguard the environment and health of its constituents.

14.6 We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  create  the  Monitoring  and
    Evaluation Team in order to strengthen and maintain  the  effectiveness
    of the LGU in being a gender-responsive Municipality.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  coordinate  with  the
Municipal Engineer in order to identify  the  projects  that  comprises  the
Construction in Progress account.  Immediately  prepare  the  Journal  Entry
Vouchers once the completed projects are identified to record  the  same  to
the  appropriate  asset  accounts.   Also,  maintain  the  Construction  in-
Progress Ledger Card (CIPLC) of each project to record the payments  thereon
from first to final payment or completion of the project.

We recommended and the Municipal  Accountant-Designate  agreed  to
conduct thorough verification on the Intra-agency Receivables  and  Payables
accounts in order to determine original entries  made  in  the  prior  years
pertaining to each account. Proper  adjusting  entries  should  be  made  to
correct erroneous entries and report the correct balances of  the  Due  from
Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  locate  all  the  missing
Disbursement Vouchers and submit the same to the  Municipal  Accountant  for
recording in the books.   Also,  stop  releasing  Disbursement  Vouchers  to
payees and other unauthorized persons and require the  payees  to  sign  the
DVs in the designated area within the premises of the Municipal  Treasurer’s
Office.


      We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  record  immediately  all
valid reconciling items that need  adjustment  in  the  books.   Also,  make
frequent representation with  the  bank  to  follow-up  the  requested  bank
records to identify the unaccounted transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

   a) enforce  the  immediate  submission  of  Utilization  Reports  by  all
      recipient NGOs/POs on funds  released  by  the  LGU  especially  those
      granted in prior years;
   b) require from the NGOs/POs proper documentation for the entitlement  of
      funds from the Municipality;
   c) initiate the preparation of a MOA indicating the  purpose,  period  of
      implementation, other basic requirements and have  the  representative
      of the recipients NGOs sign it before releasing additional funds;
   d) mandate each LGU official concerned to follow strictly the  guidelines
      on the grant, utilization and liquidation of funds by not allowing the
      grant of additional assistance without proper liquidation of the prior
      year’s grant.


      During the exit conference,  the  Municipal  Administrator  and  other
officials  concerned  who  were  present  committed   to   adhere   to   the
recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce the immediate  submission
of Utilization  Reports  by  all  recipient  barangays  on  funds  released
in the prior years. Also, direct the LGU official concerned to  locate  the
MOA covering the release  of  funds  in  the  previous  years  to  provide
documentary  proof  that  these  funds  were  actually  received  by  the
barangays.


      We also recommended that the Municipal  Accountant,  Municipal  Budget
Officer and Municipal Treasurer discontinue the release of additional  funds
to barangays which failed to submit Utilization  Reports  on  previous  fund
transfers.


      Lastly,  we  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Engineer  monitor  the
projects being implemented by recipient barangays and  assist  them  in  the
submission of the required documentary evidences on the utilizations of  the
funds.


      During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget
Officer and representative from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office  agreed  to
demand liquidation reports from the barangays and not to release  subsequent
assistance unless previous fund transfers are liquidated.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the  Disbursement
Voucher and the Municipal Treasurer prepare  the  check,  in  favor  of  the
Bureau of Treasury, to remit the  unutilized  balances  of  previous  years’
fund transfers.


      We also recommended Municipal Accountant submit immediately the Report
of Disbursement and Fund Utilization  Reports  to  the  identifiable  source
agencies on all completed programs/projects  including  information  on  the
remittance of the unused balances.


      During the exit conference,  the  Municipal  Accountant  committed  to
adhere to the recommendations, however,  it  was  informed  that  they  will
confirm first if corresponding cash is actually available for the amount  to
be returned.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  all  LGU  officials
concerned to accelerate the  completion  of  all  projects  funded  by  the
National Government Agencies (NGAs).    Otherwise,  return  to  the  source
agency the funds pertaining to projects which are  no  longer  feasible  or
could no longer be implemented for any reason/s.

We recommended that the Municipal Development  Council,  through  the
Mayor as Chairman, include  only  in  the  Annual  Investment  Plan  of  the
Municipality, projects which are investment  or  capital  in  nature.  Also,
direct the LGU officials concerned  to  fast  track  the  implementation  of
projects identified in the AIP in order to maximize the use of the 20% DF.


     We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant process for payments from the 20%  Development  Fund  only  those
identified in the AIP which are capital expenditures.


     During the exit conference, the Municipal Budget Officer agreed with
the recommendations and assured to consider it in the  next  budget  period.
However, the audit Team suggested that the CY 2018 should  be  reviewed  and
revise the same if it still included administrative expenses.

We recommended  that  the  Bids  and  Awards  Committee  (BAC)  submit
justifications why RSO Construction was awarded  with  seven  new  contracts
despite non-disclosure in its eligibility documents all  previously  awarded
contracts.

      We also recommended that the BAC scrutinize carefully the  eligibility
documents submitted by interested bidders  so  that  any  misrepresentations
will be detected and to declare as eligible only those who  strictly  comply
with the provisions of RA No. 9184.


      During exit conference, the BAC Chairman acknowledged  that  they  did
not look into all the projects in the list as long  as  there  are  projects
reported as on-going. However, they  agreed  with  the  recommendations  and
committed to look into the Statements of All On-Going Projects closely.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  and  other  LGU  officials
responsible  for  the  approval    and  payment  of  these  expenses  submit
justification  why the MDRRM Training Workshop was held  at  Malay,  Boracay
instead of conducting it within the premises of the  municipality  or  at  a
nearby location.


       We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement austerity
measures in the financial operations of LGU Leyte especially in the  conduct
of seminars and trainings outside  Region  VIII  to  prevent  incurrence  of
unnecessary and extravagant expenditures.

      These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notices
of Suspension and or Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments  of
JO wages are supported  with  the  Contracts  of  Service  for  each  JO  to
preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor,  through  the  Municipal
Accountant,  send  demand  letters  to   all   accountable   officers   with
unliquidated cash advances, especially  those  separated  from  service  and
those who are not employed in the LGU, requiring  the  immediate  settlement
of their cash advances.  Otherwise, implement the sanctions provided for  in
COA Circular No. 97-002. Also, file a request for authority  from  COA  to
write-off of the accounts of deceased employees.


      During  the  exit  conference,  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  other
officials present gave their assurance that additional  cash  advances  will
not be granted unless  previous  cash  advances  had  been  liquidated.  The
Municipal Accountant added that demand letters were already  sent  to  those
with long outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record  all  transactions
of the Municipality pertaining to taxes withheld and remitted including  the
necessary adjustments to correct the balance of  the  Due  to  BIR  account.
Also, verify the unremitted Due to BIR account balance  from  previous  year
and effect adjustments, if warranted, or remit  to  the  BIR  if  determined
valid to avoid additional incurrence of surcharges.


      We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer pay to  the  LGU  the
amount paid as penalty for late remittance of taxes withheld.


      The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer accepted the recommendation and
will trace the unsubstantiated balance from prior years’ records.

We  recommended  that  the  Human  Resource  and  Management   Officer
reconcile their records with the GSIS and  communicate  immediately  to  the
said agency any necessary change in  the  employees’  employment  status  to
update the records of the GSIS and tally with the  LGU  records  on  premium
contributions.


      We also recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  reconcile  monthly
the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS  Billing  and  against
the amount remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.


      During the exit conference, the HRMO explained that the  discrepancies
were the result during instances  of  step  increments.  Nevertheless,  they
committed to follow the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipality prioritize the establishment of a
Sanitary Landfill to achieve proper disposal of solid wastes  including  all
barangays in the Municipality.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor   invite   the   District
Supervisor, as the  legally  designated  Co-Chairman  in  the  Local  School
Board, to attend meetings of  LSB  and  submit  inputs  in  identifying  the
schools which needs improvements and assistance.

      We also recommended that the Local School  Board  incorporate  in  its
budget  programs  and  activities  intended   for   Early   Childhood   Care
Development.

      During the exit conference, the Municipal Administrator, in behalf  of
the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board  Council,  agreed  to  follow
the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all LGU officials concerned to fast-track the implementation of these projects funded by the National Government Agencies (NGAs). We also recommend that funds received for projects which could not possibly be implemented for nay reasons should be returned to the funding agency.
observations.reason: The same audit observation is noted during audit of CY 2017 transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal DRRM Officer to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM fund in accordance with its intended purpose so as to fully achieve the government's objective of appropriating the fund solely for enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the LGU as provided for the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the LGU Officials concerned implement the projects and activities identified and programmed during the year to be funded from the LDRRMF and include in the updated LDRRMIP the prior year’s unimplemented programs, projects and activities to ensure readiness of the LGU in the occurrence of disaster.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant re-classify the unexpended prior years’ DRRM funds from Due to NGAs to Trust Liabilities-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books and maintain subsidiary ledgers for these account to show breakdown of the amount for each year of transfer.

We recommended that the MDRRM Officer submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month and include in the report all information required in Annex B of COA Circular 2012-002 to enable COA and other offices concerned to conduct evaluation of the LDRRMF utilization on time.
observations.reason: Submission of Report on Sources and Utilization was not consistent.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board include as topmost priority the improvement of school facilities and other priority program/projects/activities for the use of Special Education Fund and maximize the use of the fund in accordance with the intended purpose in order to obtain the desired benefits.

We recommended that the Chairman of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat submit copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after execution/issuance thereof, to afford timely review and evaluation of the contracts.
observations.reason: Not all contracts were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to implement waste segregation at source; (b) strictly enforce and intensity the information campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents, and (c) implement programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste management program.
observations.reason: The LGU still has no sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommend (i) that the Municipal Accountant prepares a Journal Entry Voucher to record the identified reconciling items as of December 31, 2015; (ii) for the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers identified in the bank reconciliation statement unrecorded, by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant; and (iii) that the Municipal Treasurer makes certain that all disbursement vouchers, with checks already issued to the payees during the month, are in her custody, to facilitate the prompt submission of the paid vouchers to the office of the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes, thus ensuring the accurate balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA account.
observations.reason: Various items not reconciled or remained unaccounted in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensures the existence of a valid Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement and resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan prior to the transfer/receipt of funds from various government agencies, in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2012-001.
observations.reason: The same audit observation is noted during audit of CY 2017 transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the conduct of the physical inventory of all properties of the LGU by the Inventory Committee and for the preparation of the Report on the Physical Inventory of Property, Plants and Equipment. Reconciliation between the Inventory Report and the accounting records should be conducted by the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee and for the former to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect an accurate balance of the PPE accounts.
observations.reason: Completed projects recorded under Construction in Progress were not reclassified to appropriate asset accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate submission of the supporting documents required as basis in the grant of RATA. Strict compliance with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 should be observed by management to avoid suspension and disallowance of the transactions in audit.

We recommend for the submission of documents showing management’s compliance with the requirements under the Seal of Good Local Governance, and that all employees/officials obtained at least satisfactory performance rating.
We recommended that management strictly comply with the documentary requirements to support the payment of wages of laborers and Job Order employees of the municipality.
observations.reason: Accomplishment Reports are attached but lacks contracts of service this time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate settlement/liquidation of the cash advances by the accountable officers.
observations.reason: The same audit observation is noted during audit of CY 2017 transactions.

observations.recommendation: Demand Letters should be issued by the Accountant to the Accountable Officers and to enforce the sanctions that are available to the LGU for the collection of the advances. Should this be proved futile, reversion may be carried out. On the other hand, should the circumstance of dormancy of receivables prevail, write-off procedures prescribed under relevant rules.
observations.reason: The same audit observation is noted during audit of CY 2017 transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Accountant instruct all officials and employees to liquidate/settle all cash advances for travel within thirty (30) days from their return to their official station and cash advances for other activities immediately after the purpose for which it was given has been served. We further recommended that monitoring of the cash advances be made monthly by the Accountant and to issue the necessary demand letters to the accountable officer/employee.
observations.reason: The same audit observation is noted during audit of CY 2017 transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the utilization of the proceeds of the Special education Fund be limited to the prioritized projects, program and activities mandated in Section 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the submission of the (i) Program of Work duly approved by the DILG Regional Office; (ii) “No Build Zone” certification; (iii) Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing Municipal Mayor to enter into a MOA; and (iv) duly signed Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and DILG Regional Office.
observations.reason: Not all documents were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Certificate of Availability of Funds be prepared by the Accountant in compliance with the RAY Guidelines.

We recommend for the submission of the Minutes of the meeting showing the circumstances’ existing at the time of procurement which resulted in their recommendation to resort to Negotiated Procurement. We also recommend for the submission of Documents showing the approval or reason(s) for the disapproval of the Mayor on the said resolutions. We recommended for the immediate submission by the BAC of the resolutions recommending the award of the contracts to the contractor indicating the approval of the Municipal Mayor on both resolutions.
We recommend for the refund of the amount of P173, 231.00 disbursed from the Priority Development Assistance Fund by the concerned officials/personnel.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Part II No. 7
observations.reason: The Notice of Disallowance pertaining to this disbursement is on appeal to the Regional Office. Remaining balance was already returned to source.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the agency.
observations.reason: The Accountant cannot trace the amount per year of the unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive ensure for time submission of accounts monthly as mandated, in effect protect concerned personnel against the legal consequences of non-compliance.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office still incurred delay in the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant concerning the immediate submission to COA of supporting documents proving the existence of claimants to the questioned Payable accounts. After exhaustion of action towards the establishment of valid claims and no such documents are found reversion of the Payable Accounts back to the pertinent equity accounts shall follow.
observations.reason: Supporting documents to the Payables account were not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to instruct the proper recording of the financial assistance in the agency’s books using the account “Due From LGUs’ thereafter issue a written demand to all concerned barangays concerning submission of liquidation reports and other required documents. In all case however, strict adherence to the requirement of the rules on fund transfers shall be practiced for the targeted purposes of said transactions. The hereunder specific courses of actions are to be carried out: Instruct the accountant to adjust all erroneously recorded fund transfer thru the drawing of JEVs to ensure that the account “Due From LGUs” exist in the accounting records; Cause for the regular monitoring on the extent implementation on the purposes of the financial assistance; Demand for the submission of Liquidation Reports together with all supporting documents and have them turned-over for audit to obtain Credit Notice from the Audit team; and Submit Memorandum of Agreement, Work Program or Program of Activities for each of the released FA.
observations.reason: Lacking copy of MOA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that programs/projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund that were planned during the year be implemented immediately to provide the constituents there, immediate needs and for the attainment of the corresponding objectives of said fund.
observations.reason: The same audit observation is noted during audit of CY 2017 transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to exert exhaustive efforts to locate available supporting documents to prove the existence and validity of the claim against the concerned agencies, thereafter, cause for their collections. Should this be proved futile, reversion may be carried out. On the other hand, should the circumstance of dormancy of receivables prevail, write-off procedures prescribed under relevant rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should require officials concerned to submit a copy of the Purchase Order irrespective of amount, and all supporting documents thereto, to the audit team within five (5) days from issuance thereof. The Purchase Order must be eligible in all respects and should contain all the necessary data and information pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
We recommended that all government properties be provided with adequate protection from possible risk of calamities by securing a comprehensive fire insurance.

We recommend (i) the Municipal Accountant prepares Journal Entry Voucher to record the identified reconciling items as of December 31, 2015; (ii) for the immediate submission of the disbursement vouchers identified in the bank reconciliation statement as unrecorded, by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant; and (iii) that the Municipal Treasurer makes certain that all disbursement vouchers, with checks already issued to the payees during the month, are in her custody, to facilitate the prompt submission of the paid vouchers to the office of the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes, thus ensuring the accurate balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA account.
observations.managementAction: AAR was not yet effected in the CY 2015 books by the Accountant as of 12/31/16.
observations.reason: The new Accountant-Designate can not give justifications on the non-adjustment of this

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the conduct of the physical inventory of all the properties of the LGU by the Committee and for the preparation of AAR Report on the Physical Inventory of Property, Plant & Equipment. Reconciliation between the Inventory Report and the accounting records should be conducted by the former Municipal Accountant and the former to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect an accurate balance of the PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR No Report on Physical Count of Property Plant Inventory for CY 2016 was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the immediate submission of the supporting documents required as basis in the grant of RATA. Strict compliance with the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 should be observed by management to avoid suspension and disallowance of the transactions in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 4 lack supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the agency.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Part II No.10 The unexpended amount was disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Disclosures on the amount per year of transfer were not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that programs/projects be implemented immediately to provide constituents their immediate needs and attainment of the fund objectives.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Part II No. 1 Some projects were not implemented; same finding included in current report.
observations.reason: Projects remain unimplemented or partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require officials to submit a copy of the Purchase Order, irrespective of amount, and all supporting documents to the audit team within five (5) days from issuance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 Part II No. 4 Some Purchase Orders were not submitted within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Purchase Orders not submitted on time; post-audit only detected defects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Require the Provincial Accountant to strictly observe Section 59, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual for LGUs for proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale and cancelled checks. 2. Require the personnel responsible in the preparation of BRS at the Accounting Division to require the Provincial Treasurer to send notices to payees concerned on the existence of checks that have become stale and eventually revert them to the cash account.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department is still in the process of reconciling the book and bank balances and making the necessary adjusting entries for the prior years’ book reconciling items; exert effort to locate the pertinent documents necessary for the adjustment of these long outstanding reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adhere to the provisions cited in EO No. 49, DOH AO No. 163 series of 2002 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure that the items procured originated from legitimate sources and are effective as well as require submission of documentary requirements to validate the claims as well as justifications for accepting the items and effecting payments thereof despite the absence of required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: As mentioned in the AOM, the Provincial Administrator has indorsed the AOM on June 21, 2013 to various departments for appropriate action. The departments, in turn, have assured submission of required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant pursue the reconciliation
of these intra-agency receivables and payables accounts in the General Fund, Trust
Fund and Special Education Fund books to extract the correct balances of the Due
to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds in the financial statements. Also,
coordinate with the former Municipal Accountant and the LGU personnel who
previously handled the KALAHI –CIDDS projects to facilitate the reconciliation.
During the exit conference, the new Municipal Accountant-designate agreed to
coordinate with the previous Accountant in the reconciliation of the two reciprocal
accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
accelerate the implementation of projects during the year and ensure that the 20%
IRA is optimally utilized for developmental projects.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor and Administrator agreed to
follow the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all LGU officials
concerned to fast-track the implementation of these projects funded by the National
Government Agencies (NGAs). We also recommend that funds received for
projects which could not possibly be implemented for any reason/s should be
returned to the funding agency.
During the exit conference, management agreed to adhere to the audit
recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal DRRM
Officer to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM fund in accordance with its
intended purpose so as to fully achieve the government’s objective of appropriating
the fund solely for enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the
municipality.
During the exit conference, management committed to follow the audit
recommendation.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the LGU officials concerned
implement the projects and activities identified and programmed during the year to
be funded from the LDRRMF and include in the updated LDRRMIP the prior
years’ unimplemented programs, projects and activities to ensure readiness of the
LGU in the occurrence of disaster.
During the exit conference, management agreed to reprogram the prior years’
unexpended LDRRM fund in the ensuing years.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant re-classify the unexpended
prior year’s DRRM funds from Due to NGAs to Trust Liabilities-DRRM account in
the Trust Fund books and maintain subsidiary ledgers for these account to show
breakdown of the amount for each year of transfer.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the
audit recommendation.

We recommend that the MDRRM Officer submit the Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month and
include in the report all information required in Annex B of COA Circular 2012-002
to enable COA and other offices concerned to conduct evaluation of the LDRRMF
utilization on time.
During the exit conference, the LDRRM Officer-designate agreed to comply with
the reporting requirement of LDRRM funds.

We recommended that the Local School Board include as topmost priority
the improvement of school facilities and other priority programs/projects/activities
for the use of Special Education Fund and maximize the use of the fund in
accordance with the intended purpose in order to obtain the desired benefits.
During the exit conference, the Audit Team informed management regarding the
new guidelines on the utilization of SEF which was to take effect in 2017. Management
agreed to adhere to the regulations on SEF utilization.

We recommended that the Chairman of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat
submit copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5)
days after execution/issuance thereof, to afford timely review and evaluation of the
contracts.
During the exit conference, the BAC Secretariat agreed to follow the
recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) provide the necessary
support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to implement
waste segregation at source; (b) strictly enforce and intensify the information
campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents, and (c) implement
programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste management program.
During the exit conference, management agreed to adhere to the regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Provincial
Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement every month and
require the Provincial Treasurer to take charge of the disbursement of all local
government funds entrusted to him by law.

We recommended that the Provincial Accountant monitor, evaluate,
and analyze the Accounts Receivable and apprise the Provincial Treasurer of
its status to hasten its collection. Also, require the Local Chief Executives of
the different municipalities and its component barangays to submit the Fund
Utilization Report to liquidate the financial assistance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive regularly monitor the
funds received from national government agencies as well as the prosecution of
the projects in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the funds were
released and the projects were implemented. Further, require the Provincial
Treasurer to remit to the source agency or to the Bureau of the Treasury the
unused balance as of year-end.

We recommended that the Provincial Accounting Office as well as the
Provincial Government Services Office reconcile the inventory reports and
accounting records to identify the causes of the discrepancy so that necessary
adjustments can be made in the books of accounts. Likewise, the General
Services Officer should maintain and update the property cards to facilitate
reconciliation with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards
maintained by the Accounting Unit.
We also recommended that the concerned personnel in the Provincial
Accountant’s Office be required to:
a) Draw journal entries to adjust the cost and accumulated depreciation of
the transferred equipment from the General Fund Proper to the PEO
Economic Enterprise.
b) Draw journal entries to reclassify the items costing below P15,000.00
as well as the fund transferred to purchase transportation vehicles.
c) Draw journal entries to record the donated items to the Anahawan
District Hospital.
Further, the concerned personnel in the Provincial General Services
Office be required to:
a. Prepare the required inventory report immediately after the physical count
using the prescribed format and furnish a copy to the Auditor.
b. Prepare the corresponding Acknowledgment Receipt for
Equipment/Inventory Custodian Slip for the equipment/items issued to end-
users.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The Supply Officers of the different Departments/Offices to conduct
physical count of inventories on stock every six (6) months and submit
the Report on Physical Count of Inventories to the Provincial General
Services Office and to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31
of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively and to
coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the
inventory report with the Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards.
b. Require all Supply Officers of the different departments to maintain
perpetual inventory records and submit regularly the report on issuances
of inventories to the Provincial General Services Office for inclusion in
the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI).

We recommended management that the Local Chief Executive require
the Provincial Accountant to review the liability accounts and effect
adjustments thereon.

We recommended that the Provincial Accountant require the
submission of MOA and all necessary documents prior to the processing of
disbursements charged against fund transfers and thereafter submit the
liquidation report and return the unused balances after the completion of the
agreed programs/projects/activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
responsible persons to submit the necessary documentation to validate the
expenditures pursuant to Sec. 4(6) of P.D. 1445 including the justification for
non-compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-1 s. 2011. Henceforth, the 20% Development Fund should
be spent solely on development projects.

We recommended for the implementation of development projects in
accordance with the mandate of the Local Government Code and DILG/DBM
JMC 2011-1.

We recommended that the Project In-charge should:
(a) Expedite the completion of the three (3) Sanitary Landfill and Eco-
center Facility.
(b) Facilitate the construction/installation of Residual Containment Area
for the residual wastes of the province;
(c) Require strict enforcement of the “no littering” and “no open burning”
mandate of the law.

We recommended that the Provincial Accountant and the LDRRM
Focal Person adhere to the provisions under Sections 5.1.12, 5.1.13, 5.1.15 and
Section 21 of RA 10212 otherwise known as the Philippine Risk Reduction and
Management Act of 2012.
Further, we recommended to management through the LDRRM Focal
Person that in the preparation of the LDRRMF budget, the projects to be
included in the plan and budget are those that are in line with its purpose and
responsive to the need of the constituents in times of calamity instead of the
funds being tied-up with unimplemented or unfinished projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Provincial
Engineer only issue the Certificate of Acceptance after the one-year defects
liability period pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
However, the Certificate of completion should be issued upon Completion of
the project.

To avoid audit suspensions/disallowances, we recommended the
following:
a. The Provincial Accountant should see to it that supporting
documents of all transactions are complete before these are
submitted to the Provincial Treasurer for payment;
b. The Provincial Treasurer should refrain from paying claims with
lacking supporting documents pursuant to Section 4, par. 6 of P.D.
1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that all contractors be strictly required to post the
prescribed warranty securities prior to the full release of their claims on the
project.

We recommended that the Provincial Engineer attach a certification that
the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to
the prescribed agency standards and specifications pursuant to Annex A of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive refrain from granting
full Transportation Allowances to officials who are issued or are actually using
government vehicle. Full adherence to Section 59 of the General
Appropriations Act of 2016, Section VI of COA Circular No. 75-6, and Local
Budget Circular No. 103 is enjoined.
Utilization of government vehicles should be closely monitored by the
office assigned to prepare the payroll for the Representation and Transportation
Allowances. Proportionate amount pertaining to the actual days when
government transportation facilities were used should be deducted from their
monthly Transportation Allowances. We also recommend that each trip ticket
be properly accomplished and the authorized officials be required to sign as
passengers thereon.

We recommended that the investment in special savings/time deposits
be returned to the General Fund current account. Management should
immediately implement projects/programs in order that the needed services of
the constituents will be provided and not delayed or sacrificed in favor of
earning interest in time deposits. In the future, in cases where there are
identified idle funds, their investment should have the prior authority of the
sanggunian and approval of the local chief executive.
The investment in special savings/time deposits should likewise be
returned to the 20% Development Fund, 5% LDRRMF and Special Education
Fund current accounts. Thereafter, management should facilitate the
formulation of definite plans for its use and immediately implement the same.
The non-utilization or delayed utilization of available funds translates into
inadequate or delayed delivery of government services.
The Provincial Accountant must reconcile and record the transfer of the
Calamity Fund to Guinsaugon, St. Bernard, Southern Leyte so that the Cash in
Bank account of the province will be adjusted. Management should also hasten
the implementation of programs/projects for which the trust funds were
created.

We recommend that management ensures the existence of a valid Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement and resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan prior to the transfer/receipt of funds from various government agencies, in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 4 Some MOAs for other projects were not submitted.
observations.reason: Management still have to locate the MOAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the submission of documents showing management’s compliance with the requirements under the Seal of Good Housekeeping component of the Seal of Good Local Governance, and that all employees/officials obtained at least one satisfactory month performance rating to support the validity of the incentive allowance granted equivalent to one month basic salary or more.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Part II No. 5 Copy of Seal of Good Governance was not yet submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Some documents were not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the documentary requirements to support the payment of wages to hired laborers and Job Order employees of the municipality as prescribed under P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 6 The submission of the supporting documents for the payment of wages was not complied with the regulations.
observations.reason: Office supplies and other inventoriable items recorded directly as expense instead of inventory account; regulations not fully complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the utilization of the 20% economic development fund be limited to development projects, programs and activities related to social and economic development outcomes.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Part II No. 7 The same observation is noted in the CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the immediate settlement/liquidation of the cash advances by the accountable officers. Demand letters should be issued by the Accountant to the Accountable Officers and enforce the sanctions available to the LGU for the collection of the advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 8 Liquidation was effected for this amount, however, there were remained unliquidated cash advances as of 12/31/16.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instructs all officials and employees to liquidate/settle all cash advances for travel within thirty (30) days from their return to their official station and cash advances for other activities immediately after the purpose for which the advances were given has been served. Monitoring of the cash advances should be made monthly by the Accountant and demand letters issued to the accountable officers/employees.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 9 Some cash advances were liquidated.
observations.reason: Not all cash advances were liquidated within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the utilization of the Special Education Fund be limited to the prioritized projects, programs and activities mandated in Section 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 10 The same observation was noted during the CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Budget Officer and MDRRM Officer utilize the MDRRM Fund solely for projects and activities within the four (4) thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management and for the procurement of disaster equipment for disaster response and rescue activities in compliance with the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 11 No non-DRRM related transactions were charged in the CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF (MOOE & QRF) be transferred by the Municipal Accountant to the Trust Fund of the LGU in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 12 The unexpended balance was already transferred to the trust fund account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund amounting P26,760.00 be returned to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 13 The unexpended PDAF amounting P26,760.00 was transferred/returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management, through its GAD Focal Point Chairperson, submits GAD Plan and Budget for review and approval by the DILG Regional Office in conformity with the relevant provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 14 Management submitted the GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal management monitors the net take home pay of each employee/official and ensure that these are equivalent to or more than the amount required in the GAA.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 Part II No. 15 CY 2014 already implemented the required regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the submission of (i) Program of Work duly approved by the DILG Regional Office; (ii) “No Build Zone” certification; (iii) Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a MOA; (iv) Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) and supporting procurement documents including BAC resolutions and minutes.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Part II No. 2 Management submitted documents for some projects; negotiations and BAC documentation needed.
observations.reason: Some documents were not submitted to COA; procurement was via Negotiated Procurement instead of Competitive Bidding; no BAC resolution recommending award.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the refund of the disbursed amount to the concerned office.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2014 Part II No. 7 The Notice of Disallowance pertaining to this disbursement was on appeal to the Regional Office.
observations.reason: The disbursement was under appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC, with assistance of the BAC Secretariat, prepares the Annual Procurement Plan duly supported by the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of each department/office and the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as required in RA 9184 IRR.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Part II No. 8 The CY 2014 APP was submitted; APP for CY 2016 was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant records supplies using the proper inventory account and that expense be recognized whenever items are issued to end-users, supported by RIS and SSMI. Reductions of inventories shall be supported with RIS.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Part II No. 1 Some supplies and inventoriable items were already recorded in the appropriate accounts.
observations.reason: Some inventoriable items were recorded as expense during the year; records require improvement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive ensure submission of accounts monthly as mandated and cause prompt submission of documents, reports and journals for audit within the prescribed time.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Part II No. 2 The Accounting Office still incurred delay in the submission of accounts.
observations.reason: The office still had delays in submission of accounts and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for immediate submission to COA of supporting documents proving the existence of claimants to the Payable accounts. After exhaustion of action towards establishment of valid claims and no supporting documents, reversion of the Payable Accounts to the pertinent equity accounts shall follow.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Part II No. 5 No related supporting documents were submitted to COA.
observations.reason: No supporting documents; claims of doubtful validity remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommend proper use of accounts and appropriate recording of transactions to prevent non-reconciled reciprocal accounts and that adjustments be properly made on relevant records.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Part II No. 6 The same observation persisted; reconciliations were not performed.
observations.reason: Reciprocal accounts remain unreconciled; adjustments pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend proper recording of the financial assistance to the barangays as Due From LGUs; issue demand for liquidation reports together with supporting documents and submit Memorandum of Agreement, Work Program or Program of Activities for each released FA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Part II No. 7 Accountant still accounted the financial assistance in the agency’s books using the Donations account instead of Due From LGUs; demand for submissions was recommended.
observations.reason: Management has to follow up and demand liquidation reports and supporting documents from recipient barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict liquidation and settlement of cash advances by accountable officers and limit granting new cash advances until previous ones are fully settled; final demand and collection measures per COA Circular No. 2012-004 should be initiated.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Part II No. 1 Some liquidation was initiated but unliquidated advances remained.
observations.reason: Some advances remain unliquidated despite actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend timely turn-over of documents, reports and journals for audit under prescribed time and proper transmittal letters to evidence compliance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Part II No. 2 Some transactions were not submitted on time; transmittal procedures not consistently followed.
observations.reason: Transactions and documents still delayed in submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management give full attention to the GAD processes including formulation of plan, adequate budgetary allocations, identification of responsible personnel and compliance with governing laws and rules.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Part II No. 4 CY has complied with the regulations.

observations.recommendation: a. Direct concerned officials to promptly prepare the required Report on Local Sources and Utilization which shall be subject to audit by COA; b. Direct the Accountant to transfer all unexpended balances of LDRRMF to the special trust fund to protect the balance against misuse.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Part II No. 6 CY management has not fully complied with submission of Utilization Report; some transfers done.
observations.reason: Non-submission of required reports; unexpended balances were later transferred to trust fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management locate available supporting documents to prove the existence and validity of the claims against concerned agencies and cause for their collection; if collections prove futile, initiate reversion or write-off procedures as prescribed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Part II No. 8 Management initiated some collection efforts but documentation remains incomplete.
observations.reason: Supporting documents not located; collection actions ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management secure adequate protection from possible risks of calamities by availing comprehensive insurance with GSIS for exposed LGU properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2009 Part II No. 4 Management complied with regulations and secured coverage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict compliance to relevant law and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Finding Payable account to Trust Fund were set up in the General Fund & a reciprocal account in Trust Fund was likewise established. Voucher for the actual transfer of the fund is still on process. As of December 31, 2016, the paid received corresponding OR acknowledging the receipt of the actual cash.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive appoint/designate a GAD Focal Person for the Province and instructed to prepare the Annual GAD Plan for evaluation, review and assessment.
observations.managementAction: The PSWDO did not prepare the Annual GAD Plan for 2015 because the Executive Department failed to create a GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee. Despite the circumstances, the PSWDO submitted the Annual GAD Accomplishment based on CY 2014 Plan for compliance purposes because it is required by the Seal of Local Good Governance. The accounting office also has not been provided with the GAD PPA, as a guide in the implementation of GAD Programs & the preparation of Schedule of Expenses for COA verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. All procurement activities specifically the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and specifications as provided in Annex A of Revised IRR of RA 9184. A certification that the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and specifications in conformance with the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184 should be attached in the contract and that the detailed engineering outputs are adequate for the procurement at hand.
observations.managementAction: Finding AAR 2015 No. 3: BAC, in consultation with the end-user unit and/or technical experts should thoroughly consider the necessary descriptions and specifications to assure quality, durability and reasonableness of prices of the products to be delivered. They must see to it that the necessary descriptions and specifications are provided to assure quality, durability and adequate for procurement at hand. A certification that the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted according to the prescribed agency standards and specifications as provided in Annex A of Revised IRR of RA 9184 should be attached in the contract and that the detailed engineering outputs are adequate for the procurement at hand.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct concerned officials to make sure that all issuances are duly accounted and properly documented. Moreover, require the Provincial Accountant the submission of necessary documents to validate the issuances of the above discussed hospital inventories amounting to P23,004,594.32 as well as written explanation from the department concerned on why the said amount of inventories were dropped from the books despite the absence of the documents to prove the issuance to the end-user as required by the aforementioned laws and existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management has already instituted measures to comply with the recommendation by issuing Memorandum Orders to hospital supplies outlets directing them to attach also doctor’s prescriptions and proof that the patient had actually received the Drugs & Medicines or Supplies. The accounting office is ready for submission to the Office of the Provincial Accountant to validate the issuances of hospital supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive BAC to strictly comply with all the requirements of RA 9184 in all the procurement activities specifically the posting of ITB in the PhilGEPS. Likewise, for the Local Chief Executive to require the Provincial Engineer to prepare and submit the Inspection Reports upon completion of projects as required by law.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Systems Administration Officer and the BAC Secretariat have issued two certifications stating that the agency’s Digital Subscriber Line (DSL) connection was not functioning since Nov. 8, 2013 due to Typhoon Yolanda, thus failed to do the posting in PhilGEPS. While it is true that Maasin City was not badly affected by the super typhoon, however, the whole province including this city experienced total power failure for about 3 to 4 weeks. Furthermore, the management has started retrieving all the relevant documents for submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials to support all issuances of construction materials with necessary documents. Each completed project implemented by administration must be supported with duly signed Accomplishment Report, Pre and Post Inspection Report and photos (before, during and after) to document the actual repair and maintenance undertaken for each project. Likewise, we recommend for the submission of required documents to validate the projects specified herein.
observations.managementAction: Management has already attached Pre and Post Inspection Reports, Photos and various other documents to prove existence and proper implementation of its projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Provincial Accountant to submit to COA the distribution lists duly signed by the actual recipients/beneficiaries as evidences to support the actual distribution of school supplies in the implementation of the PACCO/PAIASO.
observations.managementAction: It was agreed between PACCO and PGSO that the PGSO’s transmittal date on the voucher to be processed and with signature to be prepared by the PSWDO, the end-user would follow upon further requirement of the PACCO/PAIASO. Overstatement also in quantities at Municipality of Hinunangan as reflected in the RIS and alterations in the quantities were due to the Attached List of Day Care Enrollees which was lower than the actual enrollees. Management acted accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC, in consultation with the end-user unit and/or technical experts should thoroughly consider the necessary descriptions and specifications to assure quality, durability and reasonableness of prices of the products to be procured.
observations.managementAction: The specifications for the said hospital bedside tables were prepared by the end-user. The BAC, after consultation and coordination with the end-user, adopted the specifications and the corresponding cost per unit which in the view of the BAC is already adequate and which calls for a good quality and durable bedside tables. The supplier agreed to refund an amount to the Provincial Government after the concerned authorities determine the appropriate cost of the delivered items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Provincial Accountant to submit justifications to COA on all the discrepancies noted, such as the use of questionable photos and attendance sheets, late Purchase Orders/Contracts, and to submit the lacking documents and adequate photos to prove the occurrence of activities necessitating the catering services as well as the completeness of deliveries relative thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Management will submit the necessary documents for verification to the Commission on Audit. The Accounting Department should also be required to submit a valid explanation on the utter disregard of the recommendation contained in the AOM issued by the audit team on catering services. The Provincial Accountant should submit the lacking documents and adequate photos to prove the occurrence of activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to stop the practice of splitting of contracts and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in all the procurements.
observations.managementAction: The subject BAC has now come up with measures that relative to the subject transactions would be in conformity with the provisions of law and its issuance of notice of disallowance. The BAC committed that the same issue would no longer happen again in the future.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials to comply with the requirement of Section 68 of RA 9184 in imposing liquidated damages for breach of contract, and compliance with rules and regulations provided in the Procurement Manual for Local Government Units and submission of the legal basis for the procurement of 2 units ambulance at an excessive price and other required documents.
observations.managementAction: The supplier in China was delayed in the manufacturing of 2 units ambulance, hence, delayed delivery was incurred. The said units were shipped from China on April 27, 2015 and arrived in Bauan, Batangas on May 15, 2015. As of December 31, 2016, the liquidated damages were not yet paid and likewise the supplier has not yet withdrawn its retention money.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should religiously comply with RA 9184 and other applicable laws and regulations in all its procurement activities and support them with complete and proper documentation. Likewise, the management should submit all the lacking documents to support the transactions subject in this audit finding and submit written justification for the deficiencies herein to validate the disbursements made on these contracts.
observations.managementAction: In the exit conference, the Provincial Accountant explained that the said inadequate documentation of various contracts on goods & services could have been caused by sorting error. He believed that the original copy had been signed and the one submitted to COA were duplicate copies only. He assured the team that they will look into the records and check their files and submit the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Provincial Accountant to cause the full liquidation of all cash advances to avoid audit suspensions, disallowances and the imposition of legal charges as consequence of failing to render accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management opposed on the reflected balance because according to them the liquidation documents amounting to P3,250,000.00 intended for Intelligence Fund were already submitted to COA Central Office as evidenced by the letters dated April and October 25, 2013, furnished to the Team. They informed the Team that the unliquidated balance of the cash advance in 2014 had already decreased significantly. Moreover, they promised to be more vigilant in their campaign for immediate liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of Sec. 111(2) of P.D. 1445 and related Sections of NGAS for LGU pertinent to the management and control of inventories. Require the respective departments to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies & Materials Issued (SSMI) supported by Requisition & Issue Slips/RIVs and other necessary documents depending on the nature of the transactions, duly signed by individual requisitioner/end-user for submission to the GSO. Require the submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) every semester pursuant to the above cited provisions of NGAS Manual. Require the submission of Program of Work/Bill of Materials to support the RIS and SSMI for the issuances of construction materials inventory and record them under the appropriate Construction-in-Progress (CIP) account. Then, upon completion of the project, require them to record the assets in the proper PPE accounts and require the submission of Certificate of Completion, Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection Reports and other relevant documents to prove that requisitioned items were converted into another government assets as planned.
observations.managementAction: Management acknowledged the noted deficiencies at the exit conference and they promised the Team the immediate submission of the documents mentioned above. Likewise, management commits to comply with the recommendations in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officers to observe the pertinent provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: The Provincial Administrator has already indorsed the AOM on June 21, 2013 to various departments for appropriate action. Department heads, in turn vowed to abide by the rules on proper charging of expenses.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Provincial Accountant and Treasurer to process claims strictly observing provisions of laws, accounting, budgeting, and auditing rules and regulations applicable to every transaction. Require all persons determined liable under the individual notices of disallowance to settle the disallowance immediately. Appeals to disallowances issued shall be filed/submitted following the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Memo Order was issued to the Internal Auditor informing them of the discrepancies noted in the suspensions and disallowances. Processors were made to attend a seminar/workshop on laws, rules and regulations conducted by COA to avoid disallowances in the future. In the current year, P5,947,620 of Audit Suspensions were settled while a total of P1,360.00 of disallowances were paid.

observations.recommendation: Advise the Provincial Accountant to scrutinize the composition of the accounts and carefully categorize them into a.) receivables from private persons or entities; b.) receivables from government officers and employees; and c.) receivables from other branches of the government, and analyze the individual accounts to determine their validity and feasibility of collection and send collection letters for the valid collectible accounts.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Reports as of December 31, 2011 were already submitted on February 15, 2012. As verified, part of the amounts are accounted as Due from LGUs and Due from NGOs/POs and Other Payables. However, these had not yet been fully verified due to unavailability of records.

observations.recommendation: Analyze the individual accounts to determine their validity and send collection letters for the valid collectible accounts.
observations.managementAction: Verification were already done by the Accountant and the receivables were already categorized accordingly. Part of the amount is accounted as Due from LGUs, Due from NGOs/POs and Other Payables amounting to P1,501,746.13, P60,800.12 and P2,112,664.43 respectively. However, these had not yet been verified due to unavailability of basic records to vouch their existence.
